data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the FOUNDATION story (every other story builds on it). It FULLY covers its owned slice:
- Parent AC2 clause "...using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" → fe57 AC1 (tests/fixtures/jira/*.json hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search+comment+issuelinks+parent; no tokens/emails/accountIds) + AC2 (FakeAcliClient serves them through the production fetcher enrichment path with NO shape transformation) + AC4 (fixtures consumed only via the production path; no test hand-reshapes). The round-trip contract test that consumes them is correctly delegated to sibling 8937 (which declares depends_on fe57); fe57 explicitly scopes that test OUT. FULLY contributes the real-payload-fixture half of AC2.
- Parent SC1 ("Producer (fetcher+enrichment) and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → fe57 supplies the hermetic-but-honest fixtures + verified-fake foundation flowing through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment, eliminating the bug-0ee6 root cause (differs fed hand-built shapes the real fetcher never emits). Reinforced by 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and 59ef (verified-fake shape-fidelity dual-run). FULLY contributes (foundation half).
- Parent AC1 (SSOT snapshot-entry schema) → fe57 feeds the producer path that 8937's schema pins; schema artifact owned by 8937. Contributes.

Criteria this child does NOT own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC1 schema → 8937; AC2 round-trip test → 8937; AC3/SC2 (collapse dual appliers) → 33d0; AC4/SC3 (sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary) → 2359; AC5/SC4 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode 'expected-bug' probe guards) → 822a; verified-fake honesty/dual-run check → 59ef. fe57's declared OUT items (contract test → 8937; verified-fake honesty check → 59ef) are consistent with the roster. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child fe57 itself; its owned criteria (fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc) are coherent and fully covered.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC2 (real-payload-fixtures half) + SC1 (foundation half) FULLY contributed by THIS child; AC1 contributed (schema owned by 8937); AC2 round-trip / AC3 / AC4 / AC5 / SC2 / SC3 / SC4 covered by named siblings; SC5/AC7 anti-change-detector posture applied per-test across roster; AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; already tracked on siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937 — restated here for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child, and confirmed by a repo search: no documented anti-change-detector guardrail artifact for reconciler tests exists today, and no child produces one — docs/mutation-testing.md is a general mutation-testing doc, not the AC6 governing artifact, and no child references producing/extending it):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN deliverable; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child fe57 only references the anti-change-detector concern as "a documented re-capture procedure so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors" — self-scoped to its own fixtures, not a governing guardrail for new reconciler tests; siblings 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 8937 AC4, 59ef AC3 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; repo search for an existing reconciler anti-change-detector guardrail returned no governing artifact (only general docs/mutation-testing.md). No sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future reconciler tests.
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child fe57 itself for its owned criteria (fixtures/fake foundation fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked across the sibling set.
